profile.name: Dev Chetal
profile.title: Full stack blockchain and AI engineer
profile.subtitle: I build product-grade software across AI, Web3, and full-stack systems with an eye for speed, clarity, and execution.
profile.intro: From DeFi infrastructure to AI career tools, I like turning ambitious ideas into dependable products that feel clean, fast, and ready for real users.
profile.availability: Open to select product, startup, and freelance opportunities.

stats.label: Projects shipped across AI and Web3

about.summary: I enjoy building technically ambitious products that still feel simple and intuitive when someone actually uses them.
about.detail: My background spans full-stack product development, blockchain engineering, AI experimentation, mobile work, and developer tooling. That range lets me move between architecture, interface design, and execution details without losing the product story. I care a lot about performance, readable systems, and experiences that stay calm even when the underlying logic is complex.
principles: Build with product intent, not just technical novelty.
Keep interfaces clear enough to scan in seconds.
Treat infrastructure, developer experience, and polish as one system.

points: Built decentralized exchange flows on Binance Smart Chain with smart contracts and account abstraction in mind.
Managed scalable cloud infrastructure with Kubernetes, Docker, and automation.
Delivered REST APIs and database improvements to support performance and growth.
experience.techStack: Solidity, JavaScript, Node.js, React, Kubernetes, AWS, Docker

projects.name: Resumode
projects.description: Resume analysis and ATS feedback platform with conversational guidance for improving job applications.
projects.longDescription:
Resumode is an AI-driven resume assistant that compares uploaded resumes with job descriptions and produces ATS-focused feedback. The product combines resume parsing, structured evaluation, and conversational explanations so candidates can understand what to improve instead of just receiving a score.

I approached the experience as both a workflow and a product problem: the analysis needed to feel trustworthy, the UI needed to stay simple, and the data layer needed to support repeatable uploads and feedback loops.
highlights: Resume upload and structured ATS analysis
Interactive chat workflow around career feedback
Product-focused UI built for clarity and iteration

challenges.title: Make AI feedback feel actionable
challenges.solution: Structured the output into understandable categories so users could move from diagnosis to revision quickly.

challenges.title: Balance product polish with analysis depth
challenges.solution: Kept the UI calm and progressively revealed deeper guidance only when the user needed it.

projects.name: PrepWise AI
projects.description: A career preparation experience that combines interview simulation, feedback systems, and multimodal analysis.
projects.longDescription:
PrepWise AI was built to make interview preparation more realistic and more measurable. It combines speech analysis, body-language signals, AI-generated feedback, and interactive mock interview flows to help candidates practice with more context than a static question bank.

The challenge was coordinating several ML-powered subsystems while keeping the experience usable for someone who just wants quick, credible preparation support.
highlights: Speech transcription and analysis with Whisper

challenges.title: Combine multimodal analysis into one product
challenges.solution: Built the feature set around one user flow so transcription, feedback, and posture signals felt like one experience.

challenges.title: Keep feedback helpful instead of overwhelming
challenges.solution: Turned raw model outputs into clear next-step recommendations with user-friendly language.

projects.name: AI BTC Bot
projects.tagline: Machine learning powered crypto market tooling
projects.description: Experimented with ML-driven Bitcoin analysis and trading suggestions using market data pipelines.
projects.longDescription:
This project explored how machine learning models could support crypto trading decisions by turning market data into directional suggestions. The emphasis was on experimentation, signal design, and real-time accessibility rather than building a black-box promise machine.

It was a useful bridge between backend data processing, model experimentation, and the product question of how to communicate uncertainty to users.
highlights: Applied ML in a fast-moving domain

challenges.title: Unstable, noisy source data
challenges.solution: Focused on pipeline reliability and exploratory modeling rather than overstating prediction certainty.

projects.name: UniSwap Swapper
projects.description: Automation tooling around token swaps and volume activity on top of Uniswap V3 infrastructure.
projects.longDescription:
UniSwap Swapper automates token swap flows to support repeated exchange interactions on Uniswap. The work focused on wallet orchestration, transaction execution, and making the operational behavior more reliable in a fee-sensitive environment.

This project sharpened my understanding of blockchain execution constraints, smart tooling ergonomics, and how backend automation decisions show up as user trust issues later.
highlights: Operational logic tuned for execution efficiency
DeFi automation built around actual protocol constraints
Backend services for repeatable swap activity

challenges.title: Fee-sensitive repeated execution
challenges.solution: Paid close attention to operational efficiency and wallet orchestration to reduce waste.

projects.name: EdBucks
projects.tagline: Blockchain crowdfunding for research and education
projects.description: Decentralized funding platform built on Avalanche with milestone-driven contract logic and community-facing flows.
projects.longDescription:
EdBucks explored decentralized crowdfunding around education and research use cases. The platform centered around transparent contribution mechanics, milestone-oriented smart contract behavior, and a product story that made blockchain feel useful rather than ornamental.

The project combined frontend product thinking with on-chain logic, governance ideas, and deployment considerations.
highlights: Governance thinking baked into product flows
Crowdfunding model tailored for mission-driven use cases

challenges.title: Make a Web3 concept understandable
challenges.solution: Used the product interface to explain value and flow clearly before asking users to trust the chain logic.

projects.name: Resum8 Backend
projects.description: API and authentication layer powering resume workflows, secure access, and processing services.
projects.longDescription:
Resum8 Backend was built to support the resume product layer with reliable API endpoints, authentication, and data persistence. The project focused on the operational side of product quality: secure flows, maintainable endpoints, and a backend flexible enough to support future features.

It complements the user-facing portfolio of AI resume tools by handling the system responsibilities behind them.

challenges.title: Support product growth without overbuilding
challenges.solution: Kept the API surface focused on the actual resume workflows the frontend needed.

hobbies.title: Gaming
hobbies.description: I like fast feedback loops, systems that reward mastery, and the product design lessons great games teach.
hobbies.note: A lot of my UI instincts come from how games handle flow, clarity, and momentum.

hobbies.title: Anime and storytelling
hobbies.description: I’m drawn to visual storytelling, strong worldbuilding, and character arcs with emotional payoff.
hobbies.note: It influences how I think about pacing, polish, and the feeling a product leaves behind.

hobbies.title: Learning new stacks
hobbies.description: I enjoy going deep on unfamiliar systems and getting practical with them quickly.
hobbies.note: That curiosity is a big part of why I move comfortably between AI, Web3, backend, and product work.

contact.headline: Build something ambitious, but make it feel easy.
contact.description: If you’re working on AI tools, product infrastructure, blockchain systems, or a startup that needs someone comfortable across the whole stack, I’d love to talk.
contact.availabilityNote: Best for product engineering, technical consulting, MVP builds, and systems that need both execution and taste.
